source_of_truth: draft.md contains manuscript prose; this registry is an audit and navigation layer.

objects.description: Simultaneous impact-only observed residual model u_t = B0 epsilon_t + eta_t with unit-variance structural shocks and additive residual noise; VAR lags and dynamic responses are outside the first paper. M0041 rewrote Section 2 to introduce the no-noise SVAR, recovered shocks, sign restrictions, and then the noisy model in SVAR-reader language.

For the robust transformed-cumulant route, additive residual noise is independent of structural shocks and Gaussian, so every linear transform of the noise has zero cumulants above order two. Section 4 places this assumption after the residual-noise-to-signal covariance screen and, after M55, explains why higher cumulants are blind to Gaussian noise at B0.

Scale is fixed by the user-directed M64 normalization E[epsilon_t epsilon_t']=I. The previous retained diag(B)=1 route from M54 is superseded for the active manuscript. Section 2 defines the no-noise sign set by E[e_t(B)e_t(B)']=I, and Section 4 writes the robust route as GMM over B and residual-noise variances nu.

Historical M0041 formal definition of a noisy covariance-factor pseudo-set. M64 removed the separate main-text definition and instead explains directly that B0 generally fails the no-noise unit-variance condition E[e_t(B)e_t(B)']=I under residual noise.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: No-noise sign-restricted set written as a J-test-style unit-variance condition over recovered shocks: candidates satisfy R(B) >= 0 and E[e_t(B)e_t(B)']=I. The finite-sample vector now contains two variance moments and one covariance moment.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: Historical M0041 proposition block. M64 deleted the main-text proposition as redundant and replaced it with prose explaining that any nonzero B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'} violates the no-noise unit-variance target, with off-diagonal terms also creating recovered-shock correlation.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: Historical column-rescaling obstruction from the diag(B)/rotation discussion. M64 removed this equation from the main text because the active route no longer uses the rotation paragraph or diag(B)=1 chart.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: M49 source-audited the bivariate Drautzburg-Wright higher-moment menus. GMM1 uses standardized raw products 112, 122, 1112, 1122, and 1222; GMM2 drops only the symmetric 1122 kurtosis condition. After M64, the draft presents DW as a refinement of the unit-variance second-moment set S_{J,T}(c_2), so the finite-sample set must pass both the variance/covariance block and the higher-moment block.
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md; raw/drautzburg-2023-refining-set-identification/Refining_set-identification_in_VARs_through_independence.md; manuscript/derivations/m49-dw-source-and-noisy-moment-audit.md

objects.description: Historical main-text proposition from M47. M64 removed the proposition block from the main text and replaced it with shorter prose: because DW refines S_{J,T}(c_2), residual noise can make the refined set smaller while still missing B0. The proof-audited rich-stack result remains in the derivation notes.
objects.proof_status: M47 conditionally passes the M25 proof gate: the rich-stack/ICA generic emptying result is safe with Gaussian residual noise, structural-coordinate rescaling exceptions, finite-stack alias caveats, compactness, and nonsingularity stated. The source-correct finite GMM1 row remains an implemented diagnostic rather than an unconditional independence theorem.

objects.description: Sample J-test inversion for the standard no-noise DW moment stack. After M64, the displayed set is D_T(c_2,c_S)={B in S_{J,T}(c_2): J_{S,T}(B)<=c_S}; it refines the unit-variance second-moment set rather than using a separate old B-plane covariance screen.
objects.verification_status: M25 working derivation complete; M49 source and noisy-moment audit complete; M47 conditionally passes the proof gate for compact rich-stack wording while preserving finite-GMM alias caveats. M71 rebuilds the first-shock diagnostic evidence for the post-M64 unit-variance chart after correcting the sign screen and weighting.

objects.description: M64/M66 robust DW set over the enlarged parameter vector (B,nu), with the nuisance bound represented by lambda_i=nu_i/(BB')_ii in [0,rho]. It imposes sign restrictions, the second-order block Sigma_u=BB'+diag(nu), the lambda ratio bound, and Gaussian-noise-blind higher-moment rows G_H(B,nu). M71 implements the corrected diagnostic projected evidence route by reporting accepted first-shock coordinates (B11,B21), profiling B12, B22, and lambda, imposing B11>0, B22>0, and B12<=0, and using candidate-specific pointwise covariance weights.
objects.verification_status: M64 supplies the standard GMM draft route. M66 clarifies the nuisance-bound convention and grid algorithm. M71 rebuilds Figures 1-3 and Table 1 as diagnostic pointwise-chi-square inversions after correcting the sign screen and weighting. Final projected critical values for the enlarged set remain open under M65.

objects.description: Displayed M64 moment-equation representation G_H(B,nu). It uses e_t(B)=B^{-1}u_t and parameter-implied covariance entries omega_ij(B,nu) from Omega_e(B,nu)=I+B^{-1}diag(nu)B^{-1'}, replacing the previous z(B) notation and sample covariance-product plug-ins. The full GMM vector stacks the second-order block with the five higher-moment rows.
objects.verification_status: First-pass M64 draft derivation complete. M71 implements the figure and Monte Carlo diagnostics using candidate-specific pointwise covariance weights and fixed pointwise chi-square cutoffs. Projected critical values remain open for the final inference note.

objects.description: M0035 optional covariance screen: for B=[[1,a],[b,1]] and e(B)=B^{-1}u, diagonal residual noise V=diag(nu_1,nu_2) implies E[e1 e2]=(-b*nu_1-a*nu_2)/(1-ab)^2 at the candidate truth. With 0 <= nu_i <= 0.5, the sample recovered covariance must lie in the induced candidate-specific interval.
objects.verification_status: M0035 figure diagnostic generated; the absolute bound is now a historical comparison after M0036/M40 because its upper cap is scale-arbitrary. Do not use it as the active theorem-level object without a separate audit.

objects.description: M66 unit-variance second-order screen: Psi_2(B,nu)=vech{Sigma_u-BB'-diag(nu)}=0 with lambda_i=nu_i/(BB')_ii in [0,rho], equivalently nu_i=lambda_i(BB')_ii. This replaces the old B=[[1,a],[b,1]] chart and old structural-variance nuisance parameters s_i.
objects.verification_status: Draft derivation updated in M64 and ratio notation clarified in M66. M71 implements this screen in Figures 1-3 and the Monte Carlo diagnostics with the corrected sign screen and candidate-specific pointwise weights; projected confidence-set critical values remain open.

objects.description: Historical proposition block from the pre-M64 route. M64 keeps the population validity logic in prose: at (B0,nu0), the second-order block is zero by Sigma_u=B0B0'+diag(nu0), and the higher-order block is zero by the Gaussian-noise cumulant argument. Final theorem wording is deferred to the M65 projected-critical-value note or a later proof task.
objects.proof_status: M64 first-pass derivation and M71 corrected diagnostic implementation complete. Needs projected-critical-value and proof review before theorem-level wording.

objects.description: Diagnostic statement after M71: standard-DW accepted mass outside the unit-variance robust-DW set warns that no-noise covariance-target precision may be driven by residual-noise misspecification, while a large robust-DW set records the information lost by modeling diagonal residual noise and profiling lambda.
objects.proof_status: M27 formalization complete for the comparison metric. M71 recomputed the metric bundle for the first-shock unit-variance chart after Figures 1-3 and Table 1 were corrected.

objects.description: M27 overlap metric bundle, reused in M71 on the first-shock projection chart: accepted shares, symmetric Jaccard overlap when reported, directional containment q_{S|R}=mu(S cap R)/mu(S), and warning share d_{S not subset R}=1-q_{S|R}.

M71 rebuilds the first-row residual-noise geometry under the unit-variance projected route. The displayed chart projects to first-shock coordinates (B11,B21), profiles B12 and B22, imposes B11>0, B22>0, and B12<=0, and marks whether the full true B0 passes the no-noise sign/covariance test. M72 polishes the rendered layout by using shared display limits from accepted regions and square panel boxes rather than equal data-unit aspect. M73 refreshes the figure on denser 41/11/7 default grids without changing the method or layout.
objects.output_source: M71 output: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid.png; generator manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.py --scenario-set noise; diagnostics manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.md and manuscript/simulations/output/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.json. Replication wrapper command: python manuscript/replication/run_all.py --stage figure1.

objects.description: M71 rebuilds the standard-DW residual-noise row for Figure 1 under the unit-variance projection. The standard row remains the no-noise comparator: it profiles B12 and B22 for the displayed (B11,B21) projection, imposes the maintained sign screen, and marks whether the full true B0 passes the standard no-noise and DW tests. M72 polishes the rendered layout only; it does not change masks, weights, or cutoffs. M73 refreshes the figure on denser 41/11/7 default grids.
objects.output_source: M71 output: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid.png; generator manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.py --scenario-set noise; diagnostics manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.md and manuscript/simulations/output/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.json. M49 audit: manuscript/derivations/m49-dw-source-and-noisy-moment-audit.md. Historical comparisons remain manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_relative_noise_robust_grid.png, manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_bounded_noise_robust_grid.png, and manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_pure_robust_noise_grid.png.

objects.description: M71 rebuilds Figures 1-2 under the M66 unit-variance projected (B,lambda) GMM route and the corrected first-shock chart. Figure 1 varies residual noise; Figure 2 fixes residual noise and weakens structural non-Gaussianity. Both report (B11,B21), profile B12, B22, and lambda, impose B11>0, B22>0, and B12<=0, and use candidate-specific pointwise covariance weights. M72 improves visual framing with shared display limits and square panel boxes. M73 refreshes the active figure defaults to 41 projection points, 11 profile points, and 7 lambda points.
objects.output_source: Figure 1: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid.png, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.md, and manuscript/simulations/output/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.json. Figure 2: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_unit_variance_nongaussianity_grid.png, manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_nongaussianity_grid_figure.md, and manuscript/simulations/output/sign_dw_unit_variance_nongaussianity_grid_figure.json. Generator for both: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.py. Replication wrapper commands: python manuscript/replication/run_all.py --stage figure1 and python manuscript/replication/run_all.py --stage figure2. M52 validation output remains historical: manuscript/simulations/m52_source_correct_evidence.md and manuscript/simulations/output/m52_source_correct_evidence.json.

objects.description: M71 sample-size grid under the M66 unit-variance projected (B,lambda) GMM route and the corrected first-shock chart. It fixes residual noise and structural non-Gaussianity, varies T, reports (B11,B21), profiles B12, B22, and lambda, imposes B11>0, B22>0, and B12<=0, and uses candidate-specific pointwise covariance weights. M72 improves visual framing with shared display limits and square panel boxes. M73 refreshes the active figure defaults to 41 projection points, 11 profile points, and 7 lambda points.
objects.output_source: M71 output: manuscript/figures/fig_sign_dw_unit_variance_sample_size_grid.png; generator manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.py --scenario-set sample_size; replication wrapper command: python manuscript/replication/run_all.py --stage figure3; diagnostics manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_sample_size_grid_figure.md and manuscript/simulations/output/sign_dw_unit_variance_sample_size_grid_figure.json.

objects.description: M71 corrected first-shock Monte Carlo diagnostic for the revised unit-variance GMM estimator. It uses the same corrected sign screen as Figures 1-3, reports accepted-set shares on the (B11,B21) projection, profiles B12, B22, and lambda, and compares standard-DW and robust-DW truth inclusion under candidate-specific pointwise covariance weights and pointwise chi-square cutoffs.
objects.output_source: M71 output: manuscript/simulations/m68_first_shock_evidence.py, manuscript/simulations/m68_first_shock_evidence.md, and manuscript/simulations/output/m68_first_shock_evidence.json. Replication wrapper command: python manuscript/replication/run_all.py --stage evidence. M49 audit: manuscript/derivations/m49-dw-source-and-noisy-moment-audit.md. M56 audit: manuscript/derivations/m56-robust-cumulant-gmm-generated-moment-audit.md. Superseded historical M52, M45, and M29 outputs remain for audit history.

objects.description: M78 500-replication cleaned iid sample-size Monte Carlo table for the Figure 3 DGP. It reports empirical full-B0 truth inclusion, accepted projection share as estimated-set size, and empty-set rates for Sign, DW, and nrDW, plus the warning event that DW rejects the truth while nrDW contains it across T=500,1000,2000. M78 supersedes the M74 full-grid rows by removing sample standardization, residual demeaning, recovered-shock demeaning, and sample-specific covariance weights, while using analytic iid weights W=(E[f_t f_t'])^{-1}.
objects.output_source: M78 output: manuscript/simulations/m78_clean_iid_full_sample_size_mc.md and manuscript/simulations/output/m78_clean_iid_full_sample_size_mc.json. Runner: manuscript/simulations/m78_clean_iid_full_sample_size_mc.py with --evaluation-reps 500 --projection-points 27 --profile-points 7 --lambda-points 5. Progress manifest: manuscript/simulations/output/m78_clean_iid_full_sample_size_mc.progress.json. Historical M74 output: manuscript/simulations/m74_sample_size_mc_500_grid27.md and manuscript/simulations/output/m74_sample_size_mc_500_grid27.json. Active grid/statistic code: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.py. M49 audit: manuscript/derivations/m49-dw-source-and-noisy-moment-audit.md. M56 audit: manuscript/derivations/m56-robust-cumulant-gmm-generated-moment-audit.md. M66 derivation: manuscript/derivations/m66-noise-ratio-bound-grid-algorithm.md.
objects.verification_status: M78 JSON validates and the script compiles. An independent formula-vs-observation smoke check matched raw-moment means to numerical precision before the full run. The 500-replication cleaned full-grid run reports nrDW truth inclusion of 0.884, 0.896, and 0.900 for T=500,1000,2000, with accepted projection shares of 0.063, 0.035, and 0.021. Final projected critical values remain open under M65.

objects.description: M79 500-replication cleaned iid residual-noise Monte Carlo table for the Figure 1 DGPs. It reports empirical full-B0 truth inclusion, accepted projection share, and empty-set rates for Sign, DW, and nrDW, plus the warning event that DW rejects the truth while nrDW contains it across V=(0,0),(0.2,0.2),(0.5,0.5). It uses the same cleaned iid design as M78: no sample standardization, no residual demeaning, no recovered-shock demeaning, analytic no-noise weights for Sign/DW, and candidate-specific analytic iid weights W(B,lambda)=(E[f_t(B,lambda)f_t(B,lambda)'])^{-1} for nrDW.
objects.output_source: M79 output: manuscript/simulations/m79_clean_iid_full_residual_noise_mc.md and manuscript/simulations/output/m79_clean_iid_full_residual_noise_mc.json. Runner: manuscript/simulations/m79_clean_iid_full_residual_noise_mc.py with --evaluation-reps 500 --projection-points 27 --profile-points 7 --lambda-points 5. Progress manifest: manuscript/simulations/output/m79_clean_iid_full_residual_noise_mc.progress.json. Active grid/statistic code: manuscript/simulations/sign_dw_unit_variance_noise_grid_figure.py. M78 runner: manuscript/simulations/m78_clean_iid_full_sample_size_mc.py. M77 analytic-weight formulas: manuscript/simulations/m77_clean_iid_mc_efficient_weight.py. M49 audit: manuscript/derivations/m49-dw-source-and-noisy-moment-audit.md. M56 audit: manuscript/derivations/m56-robust-cumulant-gmm-generated-moment-audit.md. M66 derivation: manuscript/derivations/m66-noise-ratio-bound-grid-algorithm.md.
objects.verification_status: M79 script compiles, quick and full-grid smoke checks passed, the 500-replication cleaned full-grid run completed with 1500 records, lambda-grid checks confirmed true lambda values for all residual-noise scenarios, and robust analytic weights required no regularization. Sign truth inclusion is 0.924, 0.140, and 0.000; DW truth inclusion is 0.846, 0.102, and 0.000; nrDW truth inclusion is 0.888, 0.880, and 0.886. Final projected critical values remain open under M65.

objects.description: M77 500-replication truth-at-B0 pointwise size audit for the sample-size DGP. It removes sample standardization, residual demeaning, and recovered-shock demeaning; draws population-normalized iid chi-square structural shocks and iid Gaussian residual noise; and uses analytic iid efficient weights W=(E[f_t f_t'])^{-1}. It reports only truth inclusion and J quantiles. M78 extends the same cleaned design to the full grid with accepted projection shares and empty-set rates.
objects.output_source: M77 output: manuscript/simulations/m77_clean_iid_mc_efficient_weight.md and manuscript/simulations/output/m77_clean_iid_mc_efficient_weight.json. Script: manuscript/simulations/m77_clean_iid_mc_efficient_weight.py. The script computes W by polynomial expansion from exact univariate raw moments through order eight, not by an auxiliary large-sample simulation.
objects.verification_status: M77 JSON validates, script compiles, and a separate 200000-draw formula check at B0 had max scaled robust covariance discrepancy about 0.070 without being used to compute W. nrDW truth inclusion is 0.884, 0.896, and 0.900 for T=500,1000,2000. M78 confirms the same truth rates on the full-grid cleaned run.

objects.description: M24 adversarial review of the robust DW higher-cumulant derivation. Conditional pass: cumulant-to-moment algebra, second-moment exclusion, normalization caveat, local rank, Gaussian-noise condition, and bias language are recorded. M37 later audited the post-M0030 diagonal-noise covariance anchor as a separate six-moment estimator gate.

Direct post-M0030 audit of the reported diagonal-noise robust DW estimator. Superseded by M0034: the off-diagonal covariance anchor is not valid in the diag(B)=1 chart unless unit shock variances are also imposed. The pure higher-cumulant part remains the active valid route under independent Gaussian residual noise.

M40 adversarial audit of the M0036 variance-ratio covariance screen. Conditional pass: the profiled covariance-decomposition algebra and implementation match the normalized bivariate chart, and the 50 percent noise-to-shock variance bound is defensible only as explicit signal-to-noise identifying information. M52 has now rebuilt lightweight figures, validation, and Monte Carlo evidence; the hard finite-sample screen still needs careful prose and final replication treatment before final claims.

Adversarial review of simulation design and interpretation for noisy sign-set bias, standard DW false sharpening, and robust DW comparison. M30 completed the first audit layer for the M35 triage script: conditional pass for exploratory use only; population-grid and final Monte Carlo audits remain.

Refreshed M28 validation gate for the M0030 revised grid pair. It is historical after M0034/M0036/M71 because the robust row used the superseded diagonal-anchor statistic. M71 supplies the active unit-variance first-shock diagnostic evidence gate.

M52 validation and Monte Carlo evidence for the M0036 variance-ratio robust DW proposal and source-correct standard-DW GMM1 comparator. The run rebuilds fixed-grid diagnostics for Figures 1-3 and a chi-square-primary Monte Carlo table with 60 calibration replications, 24 evaluation replications, and a 41-by-41 grid. It applies the hard relative covariance screen in every accepted-set and truth-inclusion calculation. The robust row uses full central-moment delta influence rows for generated fourth-cumulant moments.

M49 source and derivation audit. It establishes the exact source-correct bivariate Drautzburg-Wright GMM1 menu as 112, 122, 1112, 1122, and 1222, with GMM2 dropping only 1122; classified the then-current Figure 1/M45 standard-DW code as a historical hybrid; derives the requested noisy product moments from z=epsilon+B0^{-1}eta; and leaves a full unit-variance/rotation-chart switch as a user-decision gate. M52 later implemented the GMM1 rebuild.

M47 proof gate audit of the M25 standard-DW misspecification derivation. Conditional pass: the rich-stack/ICA generic emptying result is valid under independent Gaussian residual noise, structural-coordinate rescaling exceptions, finite-stack alias caveats, compact sign-admissible candidate sets, and nonsingularity. The source-correct finite GMM1 row remains an implemented diagnostic rather than an unconditional finite-stack theorem.

M56 generated-moment audit. It shows that robust fourth-cumulant sample entries such as mean(z1*z2^3)-3 mean(z2^2) mean(z1*z2) are smooth functions of primitive sample moments, not ordinary fixed row-level GMM moments. The population cumulant restrictions remain valid under independent Gaussian residual noise, but final finite-sample evidence should use primitive-moment delta-method covariance including centering terms, an equivalent augmented nuisance-covariance GMM system, or bootstrap/repeated-sample calibration. M71 implements the active first-shock diagnostic evidence with candidate-specific pointwise covariance weights; final projected critical values remain open.
objects.source: manuscript/derivations/m56-robust-cumulant-gmm-generated-moment-audit.md; KnowledgeVault/vault/papers/A Generalized Method of Moments Estimator for Structural Vector Autoregressions Based on Higher Moments.md; KnowledgeVault/svar-toolkit/docs/api/gmm.md
